methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Claros operates in Climate & Energy at the Seed stage, having raised $30M to date. The sector comprises nine peer companies—one public (LNZA) and eight private—including later-stage entrants such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. A narrow peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the Climate & Energy sector; the peer set lacks sufficient depth at Seed valuation levels to support a reliable multiple-based triangulation.

The stage-progression method applies directly to this dataset. Using $30M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $75M. This method is grounded in historical financing patterns for early-stage frontier-tech companies transitioning from Seed rounds.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $64M to $86M, centered on $75M. This range reflects ±15% variance around the stage-progression median to account for execution risk and the absence of direct peer benchmarking. Key uncertainties include technology de-risking progress, capital efficiency relative to sector norms, and the magnitude of step-up multiples in a potentially shifting late-stage climate-tech funding environment. A peer-median method should be revisited once additional Seed-stage or Series A climate companies with disclosed valuations become available.
